name: Pico del Rayo — circular por la Sierra de Victor desde el Parador de Calatayud
region_intro: Mountain biking around Calatayud offers diverse and striking natural landscapes, making it an excellent destination for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by unique "Far West" landscapes in the Sierra de Armantes, featuring gypsum steppes, extensive pine forests, and distinctive red sandstone formations. Riders can explore dry ravines with sandstone and gypsum walls, alongside challenging ascents to peaks like Pico del Rayo. This varied terrain provides a wide spectrum of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Calatayud The most popular mountain bike trail is Far West Bilbilitano — circular por la Sierra de Armantes desde Calatayud, a 27.6 miles (44.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the unique "Far West" landscapes of Sierra de Armantes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pico del Rayo — circular por la Sierra de Victor desde el Parador de Calatayud, a difficult 19.4 miles (31.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain as it ascends Pico del Rayo, offering panoramic views. Local mountain bikers also love the The Castles loop from Calatayud, a 26.7 miles (43.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 16 minutes. Mountain biking around Calatayud is defined by "Far West" landscapes, gypsum steppes, and challenging climbs to peaks like Pico del Rayo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from less technical singletracks to demanding circuits. The routes in Calatayud are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calatayud's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Calatayud?
A: There are nearly 90 mountain bike trails around Calatayud listed on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 13 easy routes, 51 moderate routes, and 25 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Calatayud?
A: Calatayud's mountain biking terrain is incredibly diverse. You'll encounter unique "Far West" landscapes, particularly in the Sierra de Armantes, featuring gypsum steppes, extensive pine forests, and distinctive red sandstone formations. Routes also include dry ravines with sandstone and gypsum walls, as well as challenging climbs to peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2612188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Rayo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Calatayud?
A: Yes, Calatayud offers a spectrum of trails suitable for various skill levels, including options that are less technical and more fun, making them suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, the overall network includes easier options that can be enjoyed by riders of different ages and abilities.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while mountain biking in Calatayud?
A: Beyond the unique "Far West" landscapes of Sierra de Armantes, you can encounter impressive natural features and historical sites. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Far West Bilbilitano Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;, and challenging ascents to peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2612188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Rayo&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts the stunning Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park with its waterfalls, and the dramatic Desfiladero del Río Mesa gorge, though these may require a short detour from specific bike routes.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Calatayud?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Calatayud are designed as circular trails, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984977265/far-west-bilbilitano-circular-por-la-sierra-de-armantes-desde-calatayud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Far West Bilbilitano — circular por la Sierra de Armantes desde Calatayud&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984992564/pico-del-rayo-circular-por-la-sierra-de-victor-desde-el-parador-de-calatayud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Rayo — circular por la Sierra de Victor desde el Parador de Calatayud&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Calatayud?
A: The mountain bike trails around Calatayud are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique landscapes and the range of options for different abilities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Calatayud?
A: Calatayud is suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding. While winter mountain biking is possible, be prepared for cooler conditions. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred during this season.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders looking for longer distances and significant elevation gain, Calatayud offers several challenging routes. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38237301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castles – Calatayud loop from Calatayud&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 46.3 miles (74.6 km) with over 1400 meters of elevation gain, typically taking around 7 hours 30 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there options for mountain bikers who prefer less technical trails?
A: Absolutely. While Calatayud is known for its challenging routes, there are also less technical, fun singletracks with numerous curves and berms. These trails are described as "poco técnico pero muy divertido" (not very technical but very fun), providing enjoyable options for those who prefer a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are the typical durations for mountain bike trails in Calatayud?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984977265/far-west-bilbilitano-circular-por-la-sierra-de-armantes-desde-calatayud" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Far West Bilbilitano — circular por la Sierra de Armantes desde Calatayud&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 27.6-mile (44.5 km) route, typically takes around 5 hours 3 minutes. Shorter, easier routes will naturally take less time, while longer, more challenging loops can extend to 7 hours or more.

Q: Is Calatayud easily accessible for cyclists from other cities?
A: Yes, Calatayud boasts a strategic location and good accessibility, including an AVE high-speed train stop. This makes it a convenient base for cyclists traveling from other major cities, allowing for easy arrival and departure to enjoy the region's mountain biking opportunities.
tips: El Frasno shelter, picnic area and public fountain. This refuge is an ideal place for hiking and nature lovers.
Or refuge of Inogés, it is a free refuge in quite good conditions. We must take them into account in case we had to take shelter there from a good storm
The peak of Rayo with 1,427 meters of altitude, is the highest peak of the Sierra Vicor. It is the second highest peak in the Iberian System of Zaragoza after Moncayo.
